title: ADR-0001 owns test-harness/pure-logic coverage for local-seo-agency
content: Settled decision (Accepted 2026-05-27): docs/adr/ADR-0001-test-harness-and-pure-logic-coverage.md. Runner = node --test via tsx. Two verified facts to reuse: (1) format.ts formatSignedCurrency uses typographic minus U+2212 (−), NOT ASCII hyphen — any test asserting '-$...' fails spuriously. (2) Rankings has NO pure computation module; the only rank math is inline in KeywordTable.tsx and carries a real defect — rank drops (now>prev) render as '—' identical to no-change. Map-pack/share-of-voice are pure presentation, nothing to unit-test. As of 2026-05-27 the plan remains UNIMPLEMENTED (no test script, no tsx, no rank.ts, no test files). Extend this ADR; do not re-derive it.

Seeded conservative defaults: autonomy_level=review_each_pr, max_concurrent_prs=1, release cadence weekly Friday 17:00. These are unconfirmed by the user — confirm before relying on them. ADR-0001 (test harness) was handed off as 5 backlog candidates (11 pts) and is sitting in build_queue.json as pending_triage, NOT accepted; nothing implemented because no explicit triage accept was given. The architecture handoff event (architecture.analysis.completed) is treated as a candidate source, not an implementation trigger.

title: ADR-0001 candidate t4 changes UI — rank-drop defect at KeywordTable.tsx:43
content: Verified business-relevant defect in Local SEO Agency dashboard: rankings table at src/features/rankings/components/KeywordTable.tsx:43 renders rank DROPS (now>prev) identically to no-change ('—'), because the branch is `change > 0 ? '↑ N' : '—'`. A keyword that lost ranking looks unchanged to the user — a real correctness bug, currently invisible only because no down-move row exists in the static mock data. The ADR-0001 fix (candidate t4) makes drops render as '↓ N', which changes rendered dashboard output and must be explicitly approved as a UI change. Also: format.ts uses typographic minus U+2212 in signed currency and leaves NaN unguarded in formatPercent.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-27 12:18 UTC
ADR-0001 triage re-presented — still pending_triage, no accept received
content: On 2026-05-27 the dev-clone.backlog.candidate event for ADR-0001 (xkazm04/xprize-local-seo-agency, 5 candidates / 11 pts) fired again and was routed to uc_triage. Candidates were re-presented to the human for accept/reject/defer via the review surface; no implementation was started because no explicit per-item triage accept has been recorded yet. Items remain in build_queue.json as pending_triage. t1 (wire test runner) is the prerequisite for t2/t3/t4. t4 is the only UI-changing item (rank-drop '↓ N'). t5 (NaN-guard) requires a product decision. Until the human resolves the review, the build queue stays blocked — this is correct behavior, not a stall.

title: Resolved/open code-review findings — ADR-0001 (local-seo-agency)
content: Code review on 2026-05-27 of ADR-0001 targets, verdict REQUEST_CHANGES. Open findings by signature (do NOT re-flag if user marks resolved): [cr-f1] HIGH KeywordTable.tsx:42 — rank drops render identical '—' to no-change because only change>0 handled (change=prev-now); hides ranking losses, the dashboard's core signal. Latent in mock data (all rows gains/flat). [cr-f2] MED format.ts:30 formatPercent passes NaN/Infinity through → 'NaN%'. [cr-f3] MED format.ts:20 formatSignedCurrency → '$NaN' on non-finite. [cr-f4] LOW format.ts:21 mixes ASCII '+' with U+2212 '−'. Scope note: formatSignedCurrency + formatPercent have ZERO call sites in src/ — format findings are latent until wired to live data. Upstream mis-cited the table defect as :43; actual line is :42.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-27 12:20 UTC
Security baseline — local-seo-agency (scan 2026-05-27)
content: First security scan of local-seo-agency (Next.js 16.2.6 / React 19.2.6 / TS). All 3 phases ran clean coverage. OPEN findings (dedupe signatures; do NOT re-flag if marked resolved): [sec-m1] MEDIUM dependency — postcss <8.5.10 GHSA-qx2v-qp2m-jg93 (XSS via unescaped ) bundled transitively under next@16.2.6 at node_modules/next/node_modules/postcss; direct postcss devDep ^8.5.15 already patched; fix = npm overrides pinning next>postcss>=8.5.10, NOT the audit-suggested next@9.3.3 downgrade. [sec-m2] MEDIUM injection — src/app/page.tsx:66-67 dangerouslySetInnerHTML on STATIC literals (renders < entity); latent only, no user input today; fix = render plain JSX text. CLEAN: no hardcoded secrets in src/, .env/.gcp/*.pem properly gitignored & untracked; no eval/exec/raw-SQL/process.env leak; no API routes so no auth/PII/server-injection surface. Note: inbound Code-Reviewer findings (KeywordTable.tsx:42, format.ts) are correctness/quality, out of security scope.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-27 12:23 UTC
Version decision — local-seo-agency v0.2.1 (PATCH, docs-only)
content: Released v0.2.1 on 2026-05-27, bump 0.2.0→0.2.1 PATCH. Rationale: only commit since the v0.2.0 tag (8f2a019) was documentation-only (ADR-0001, backlog brainstorm md+json, moonshots-2026-05-21, README tech-stack refresh) — no src/, dependency, or API change. Repo convention is PRE-1.0: breaking→MINOR, everything-else→PATCH (stated in CHANGELOG). Tag v0.2.1 + commit 2edefbd created locally, NOT pushed. Prior context: v0.2.0 was the Next14→16 / React18→19 / Tailwind3→4 / TS5→6 breaking-platform migration (a MINOR under 0.x). Note for next run: the v0.2.0 tag points to commit 2cd0814 where package.json still read 0.1.0; the 0.2.0 version/changelog bookkeeping landed one commit later (8f2a019) — tags can lag the version-file bump in this repo.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-27 12:25 UTC
v0.2.1 was a docs-only release, already in sync
content: The local-seo-agency v0.2.1 release (commit 2edefbd) contained only documentation changes: ADR-0001, backlog brainstorm (md+json), moonshots planning set, and a README tech-stack refresh to the 0.2.0 platform (Next.js 16, React 19, TypeScript 6, Tailwind CSS 4, ESLint 10). The Release Manager writes the CHANGELOG entry inside the release commit itself, so the Docs Steward often finds the changelog already current at release.published time — the steward's job becomes verification, not authoring. No functional/user-facing code changes shipped in 0.2.x patch since 0.2.0. Last-processed commit: 2edefbd.
